title: AIADMK overhauls governing council, key committees ahead of 2026 Tamil Nadu Assembly election

title: Rahul writes to PM Modi, seeks Bharat Ratna for BSP founder Kanshi Ram

title: Bhupinder Hooda confident of Congress win in Rajya Sabha polls in Haryana

title: Fisheries sector in Puducherry to get major infrastructure boost with projects worth ₹246 crore

title: Environment Ministry endorses names of 10-member expert committee set up to define Aravalli hills
content: In an affidavit filed before the top court, the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has endorsed the names suggested for the high-powered expert committee comprising in-service and retired bureaucrats associated with the Forest Survey of India (FSI), Geological Survey of India (GSI) and Survey of India, along with academicians.

title: Burglary at Kowdiar Palace; jewellery worth ₹2 crore stolen
content: According to the complaint, several pieces of gold and gem-studded jewellery, including antique valuables, that were kept in an iron almirah in her bedroom at the palace had gone missing

title: Resign if you can’t maintain law and order: Allahabad HC to SP, DM on Sambhal mosque prayer row
content: The petition challenged the order that limited the number of people permitted to offer namaz at the mosque. During the hearing, the court observed that if the local administration is unable to maintain law and order, officials concerned should step aside.

content: According to preliminary information, the two-wheeler had undergone engine boring on Saturday (March 14, 2026) and was reportedly left running overnight inside the house on the mechanic’s advice

title: Two killed, six injured as wall of house collapses in Bihar
content: According to villagers, the wall of a house where the wedding ceremony was going on suddenly collapsed on them, Obra Police Station SHO Nitish Kumar said.

title: Trinamool MP Mahua Moitra says BJP workers brought ‘jungle raj’ in West Bengal
content: Mahua Moitra criticised BJP workers for vandalising and attacking Bengal Minister Shashi Panja’s house, adding that no political party has ever vandalised the house of a Cabinet Minster in the State

content: The Congress frequently uses the term 'G2' to take swipes at Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Home Minister Amit Shah, who are both from Gujarat.

title: Odisha BJP shifts its MLAs to Paradip ahead of Rajya Sabha polls

title: In frames | War lights a kitchen fire
content: West Asian conflict has snuffed out a clean cooking fuel in kitchens; facing a crisis, homes and eateries are looking at firewood and biomass to energise their cooking

title: Watch: HPV vaccination roll out, climate change, metabolic diseases and more | Health Wrap
content: In this episode of Health Wrap, senior health journalists Ramya Kannan and Zubeda Hamid discuss the week’s important developments in public health. This episode looks at the rollout of Human papillomavirus (HPV) vaccination across States in India to prevent cervical cancer, which continues to claim thousands of lives each year.

title: ECI to announce Assembly poll dates for four States, one Union Territory today
content: The poll authority has convened a press conference at 4 p.m. to announce the schedule for Assam, Kerala, West Bengal, Tamil Nadu and the Union Territory of Puducherry

title: Fire breaks out at Nature Bazaar in south Delhi, around 50 shops gutted
content: According to the DFS, a call regarding the blaze was received at 7.37 a.m., following which 10 fire tenders were rushed to the spot to control the flames. No injury to anyone has been reported so far.

title: India well-placed to deal with any situation on crude oil, fuel amid West Asia supply shock: Piyush Goyal
content: Acknowledging that while the country is facing "stress" on the rupee, Mr. Goyal said there is no reason for anyone to panic as rupee is expected to bounce back in a more gradual fashion, possibly once the war is over

title: Fact check: Are “40 out of 100 children malnourished” in Gujarat?
content: Data indicates that the statement is true and that several of the worst affected districts are tribal ones, pointing to a higher burden of malnutrition among adivasi communities in the State

title: Gateway to the world, from Madurai

Sundar traces the long history of such a demand and highlights what the new status will mean for the ever-growing tourism, connectivity, and industrial needs of districts in southern Tamil Nadu

title: Madhavaram Retteri lake set to have boating, tourism facilities in a few days

title: BJP indulging in horse-trading to win Rajya Sabha election; former CM Naveen Patnaik
content: All political parties are conducting mock-poll training for their MLAs to ensure that their votes are not rejected

title: ‘Pandit’ option for question on opportunists trips up BJP in U.P. days after ‘anti-Brahmin’ claim by opposition

title: Close fight on the cards in Kerala’s bellwether constituency — Ollur
content: Since 1982, Ollur has elected candidates from the front that eventually forms the government in the State. Urban-rural mix, strong religious influence, and shifting voter loyalties make the constituency politically complex

title: Jeweller arrested in IDFC First Bank fraud case in Haryana; 12th arrest
content: The case pertains to private lender IDFC First Bank, which had disclosed a ₹590 crore fraud involving certain employees and others at its Chandigarh branch in a set of accounts held by the Haryana government

title: Why did the SC allow passive euthanasia? | Explained
content: What test did the Supreme Court apply in the Harish Rana case to decide if continuing treatment was in his best interest? What nuanced distinction did it provide between active and passive euthanasia? What have been India’s past positions on the right to die?

title: Mojtaba Khamenei | The cleric with a gun
content: A wounded man who lost his parents and wife, and was elected the ‘Supreme Leader’ of a nation under attack from the world’s most powerful country and its closest ally, the ayatollah now faces a rare crisis his predecessors never confronted — to survive the storm and preserve the republic

title: Experts laud A.P. CM’s vision for Quantum Valley project

title: CM Stalin writes to PM to increase rice procurement
content: In a letter to Mr. Modi, he said the timely opening of the Mettur Dam for irrigation, favourable climatic conditions, increased area coverage, improved crop management practices, better irrigation support, and the adoption of high-yielding varieties of paddy by farmers

title: International status for Madurai airport will draw more tourists, pilgrims, says Union Minister
content: Formally according the status to Madurai airport, Civil Aviation Minister Kinjarapu Rammohan Naidu says the move will also help create more jobs and greatly boost hospitality, MSME, and agricultural sectors in the district; he also inaugurates new air traffic control tower set up at cost of ₹94 crore

title: Varsity created needless controversy, says renowned feminist historian Uma Chakravarti
content: ‘The historical roots of patriarchy need to be understood. We need to study as well, because we have to substantiate our point of view. Changes have happened when we have understood changes,’ she said

title: Roadside eateries in Vijayawada worst hit by LPG crisis
content: Street vendors and owners of carts are struggling to keep their stalls running, while several others have either cut down their operations or closed temporarily

title: India at a crossroads, next step to define country’s future: Kamal Haasan
content: At the book launch of author and former West Bengal Governor Gopalkrishna Gandhi’s India and Her Futures, actor-MP Kamal Haasan said the question before our country is whether we join the chorus of nations exporting division as politics or remain true to the extraordinary, ethical, and moral imagination that created India

title: Transcultural exchanges have long shaped Kannada literature: Shivanand Kelaginamani
content: The professor says the long history of the Kannada language can be traced through classical works such as Kavirajamarga; he notes that elements from Sanskrit were gradually incorporated into Kannada.

title: India facilitating negotiations within BRICS on West Asia conflict: Jaiswal
content: Acknowledgement of intra-BRICS negotiation comes even as External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar continues his telephone calls with his Iranian counterpart Seyed Abbas Araghchi and other regional diplomats

title: Why Indian airlines are among the worst hit by the Gulf conflict
content: Almost four crore people flew between India and the Gulf in 2025; half of all international passengers flying to or from India was on a Gulf route; idle aircraft, longer flights, higher costs, lower revenue all hurting Indian carriers

title: Kalaburagi Rangayana to organise month-long summer theatre workshop for children
content: Rangayana director Sujata Jangamshetty said the workshop aims to help children explore their creative abilities, understand theatre art and improve their personality development.

title: Ladakh to protest for safeguards on March 16, despite Sonam Wangchuk release
content: Struggle for rights continue, say civil society groups while demanding release of two more detained since September 2025; Opposition questions Centre over the prolonged detention of the activist and says PM should apologise to people of Ladakh

title: LPG crunch puts strain on mid-day meals for children at schools across India
content: Many schools are cutting down on their daily menu, affecting the nutritional quality of the food being provided to the children; reports indicate that as schools apply for gas cylinder refills, the situation may become dicey

title: Chief Minister M.K. Stalin meets Deva and Jiva, the child influencers who tell everyone to study
content: The brothers, natives of Sathipattu near Panruti in Cuddalore district, are known figures on social media through their reels over the last one-and-a-half years

content: Karnataka has framed Karnataka Women Well Being Leave Bill, 2025, which is yet to be tabled in the Legislatur

title: Karnataka prisons to introduce drug tests for inmates to curb smuggling of narcotic substances
content: Under the initiative, inmates will be tested for substances such as ganja, heroin and cocaine. In the first phase, a group  of prisoners in all jails will undergo screening

title: Immensely pleased to celebrate JSP Formation Day in remote tribal area, says Pawan Kalyan
content: He unfurls the party flag in the presence of tribal communities at Onuru Junction and later walks  for about 2.4 km to inspect the road laid under the PM JANMAN scheme

title: Our emphasis is to promote raw talent, Union Sports Minister Mandaviya
content: The Union Minister says that the sports sector has opened up umpteen opportunities for youngsters to build their careers in the field.

content: The RedHawks and Tigers continue to be the talk of the bubble, plus more considerations for men's committee.

title: Duke shows mettle in holding off UVA for ACC title
content: No. 1 Duke defeated No. 10 Virginia on Saturday night for its second straight Atlantic Coast Conference tournament championship under coach Jon Scheyer and its third title in the past four years.

title: Venezuela KO's defending champ Japan from WBC
content: Wilyer Abreu hit a go-ahead, three-run shot in the sixth, and Venezuela rallied to stun defending champion Japan to advance to the World Baseball Classic semifinals.

title: Reaves, Luka deliver late-game magic in OT thriller
content: Austin Reaves executed a perfect missed free throw before sinking the tying bucket to force overtime, and Luka Doncic drained the winning jumper in the extra frame to lift the Lakers past the Nuggets on Saturday night.

title: Arizona gets revenge, tops UH to win Big 12 title
content: Arizona will head into the NCAA tournament with a nine-game winning streak after holding off Houston 79-74 in Saturday's Big 12 tournament final, avenging its loss to the Cougars in last season's conference title game.

title: St. John's routs UConn to defend Big East title
content: A tenacious defensive performance carried No. 13 St. John's to its second straight Big East tournament championship Saturday night with a 72-52 blowout of sixth-ranked UConn at Madison Square Garden.

title: Leafs want Gudas punished more for Matthews hit
content: The Maple Leafs believe the NHL didn't go far enough in punishing Ducks defenseman Radko Gudas for the hit that ended Auston Matthews' season.

title: TTU to have star Anderson available for tourney
content: Texas Tech star guard Christian Anderson will be available for the NCAA tournament after leaving the Red Raiders' Big 12 tournament loss because of an apparent groin injury.

title: Wemby's do-it-all showcase propels red-hot Spurs
content: Victor Wembanyama made his mark in every facet of Saturday's game against the Hornets, posting 32 points, 12 boards, eight assists, four blocks and two steals as the Spurs improved to 17-2 since Feb. 1.

content: Manchester United are reported to be leading the way to sign Bournemouth and USMNT midfielder Tyler Adams. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Cheat sheet: Sleepers, busts, rookies and closers
content: You've read their names all preseason long, it'd be a shame to forget them on draft day. The ESPN Cheat Sheet is one way to make sure that doesn't happen.

title: 120 kmph In Hyundai Verna At Night, Shooting Speeding Reels. 2 Die In Crash
content: Shortly before the accident, the man in the driving seat, Avadh Tiwari, had posted videos of him speeding the car, reportedly a Hyundai Verna, at about 120 kmph on Instagram.

title: Omar Abdullah Shares Glimpses Of 'Asia's Largest Tulip Garden' In Srinagar
content: Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah shared glimpses of "Asia's largest tulip garden in Srinagar" which he said looked vibrant even before the full bloom. He appreciated gardeners and the Floriculture Department team for their hard work.

title: Election 2026 Date Announcement LIVE: Poll Dates For 5 States To Be Announced Today
content: The Election Commission will today announce dates for assembly polls in five states. Polls are due in West Bengal, Keralam, Tamil Nadu, Assam and Puducherry.

title: 'Not Targeted At Any Community': BJP's Assam Poll In-Charge On Evictions
content: Assam Assembly Election: The BJP-led Assam government has initiated several extensive eviction drives, citing claims of illegal land encroachment.

title: AIADMK Chief Palaniswami Rules Out Alliance With Vijay-Led TVK
content: AIADMK general secretary Edappadi K Palaniswami has ruled out an alliance with actor-politician Vijay-led fledgling TVK for the upcoming Assembly polls and dismissed the scope for an alliance as mere media speculation.

title: Bengaluru Man Sends Obscene Texts To Bank Staffer After Missed Call Request
content: A case has been filed against a Bengaluru man for allegedly sending obscene and inappropriate messages to a woman working at a State Bank of India branch.

title: North Korea Tests Nuclear-Capable Rocket Launchers With Kim, Daughter In Attendance
content: Kim Jong Un said the drill would expose "the enemies within the 420-km striking range to uneasiness".

title: 4 Of Family Die In Andhra After Inhaling Fumes From Bike Kept On All Night
content: Four members of a family died due to asphyxiation after inhaling smoke from a bike kept on for the entire night in Andhra Pradesh's Annamayya district, police said on Sunday.

title: Who Covers AI Business Blunders? Some Insurers Cautiously Step Up
content: The trend of "agentic AI" has taken off, with bots handling computer tasks by themselves and businesses trimming ranks of human workers as a result.

title: US Military Could Use AI Chatbots For War Planning, Palantir Demo Suggests
content: US defence contractor Palantir has shown how artificial intelligence chatbots could help military analysts review intelligence and generate possible battlefield plans, according to software demos and Pentagon records.

title: "It's Safe, Will Always Be Safe": Dubai's Push To Contain Fallout From Iran War
content: Iran has fired over 1,800 missiles and drones at the UAE, more than any other country targeted by Tehran in the war.

title: Ex-BRS MLA Pilot Rohith Reddy's Farmhouse Raided, 5 Test Positive For Drugs
content: Former BRS MLA Pilot Rohith Reddy was one of the five people present at the party who tested positive in the drug test.

title: Loan From UK-Israeli Firm Funded Mojtaba Khamenei's London Homes: Report
content: Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei used a 36 million UK poundsloan from a company owned by British-Israeli businessmen to help finance the purchase of luxury homes in London.

title: Explained: Why Did It Rain In Delhi Today And Will It Be Hot Again Next Week?
content: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) issued an orange alert for the national capital, warning of moderate rain, thunderstorms, lightning and strong winds through the day.

title: Man Arrested For Harassing, Abusing Kerala Woman At Hostel In Bengaluru
content: The incident is reported to have taken place on March 11 between 8:50 pm and 9:50 pm at a popular hostel chain located in the Indiranagar area.

title: HIV-Positive Man Injects His Blood Into Woman For Cancelling Wedding
content: A 24-year-old man allegedly administered HIV-infected blood to a 22-year-old woman when her parents called off her wedding with him after he tested positive for the contagion, police said on Saturday.

title: Madurai Airport Gets International Status, Tuticorin Now Thoothukudi Airport
content: Union Civil Aviation Minister Ram Mohan Naidu formally renamed Tuticorin Airport as Thoothukudi Airport in Tamil Nadu, saying that the renaming corrects a colonial legacy and re-establishes the true strength of our culture, heritage, and languages.

title: UP Man Kills Father With Brick Following Argument While Drinking
content: A 55-year-old man was allegedly bludgeoned to death with a brick by his son following a dispute while they were consuming alcohol here, police said on Saturday.

title: Punjab man shot dead in Canada: How passenger pickup from motel ended in fatal shooting
content: A 30-year-old Punjabi cab driver, Devinder Singh, was tragically shot and killed in North Battleford, Canada, while on a pickup. Police are investigating the homicide, which occurred when a man reportedly followed a female passenger and opened fire. Devinder, who had moved to Canada four years ago, was a driver for Crown Cab and co-owned a café with his wife.

title: Rahul Gandhi demands Bharat Ratna for BSP founder Kanshi Ram; writes to PM Modi
content: Rahul Gandhi has urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to posthumously award the Bharat Ratna to Bahujan Samaj Party founder Kanshi Ram. Gandhi highlighted Kanshi Ram's significant role in social justice and empowering marginalized communities. This call follows consistent demands from Dalit leaders and intellectuals for the highest national honor for the BSP founder.

title: ‘Child trusted him as a grandfather’: Mumbai court convicts 79-year-old man in Pocso case
content: A 79-year-old man was sentenced to three years of simple imprisonment for sexually assaulting a 10-year-old girl. The court found the accused guilty of luring the child into his home and kissing her, deeming her testimony reliable despite defense claims. The judge considered the accused's advanced age when awarding the sentence.

title: Perplexity AI CEO agrees that Computer Science is gradually returning to its roots
content: AI is rapidly automating coding's routine tasks, pushing software engineering back towards its mathematical roots. Experts predict AI could soon handle most end-to-end coding, with some engineers already code-free. While LLMs still struggle with complex design, this shift is prompting a rethink in computer science education, emphasizing logic over syntax.

title: Honour killing: Couple murdered by woman’s father, cousin in Gujarat
content: A young couple's disappearance in Gujarat has been revealed as a brutal honor killing. The 19-year-old woman's father allegedly orchestrated their murders because he opposed their marriage. Police recovered the decomposed bodies from a well, leading to the arrest of the father and his nephew.

title: ‘Good stocks in hand’: India well placed on crude, fuel despite Middle East war, says Goyal
content: India is well-prepared for Middle East crisis disruptions, with ample fuel stocks and no supply issues reported.  The government is diversifying gas import sources and will soon announce support for affected exporters, including insurance schemes.  Despite short-term economic pressures and rupee stress, India remains confident in its growth trajectory.

title: Who was Madhu Malhotra? 'Satte Pe Satta' and 'Hero' actress who passed away at age of 71
content: Beloved star Madhu Malhotra, recognized for her captivating performances in classics like 'Satte Pe Satta' and 'Hero', sadly departed at the age of 71 on March 13. With a prolific career that flourished during the 80s and 90s, she graced over 100 films, leaving a lasting impact with standout roles in both 'The Great Gambler' and 'Satte Pe Satta'. Read on to know more in detail.

title: Accenture CEO Julie Sweet to company’s employees: If you want a promotion, you need to ...
content: Accenture CEO Julie Sweet has mandated that employees must demonstrate proficiency in using AI tools to be considered for promotions. This requirement reflects the consultancy's integration of AI into its core operations, a shift Sweet likens to the adoption of computers in the past. The company has invested significantly in reskilling its workforce to adapt to these evolving workplace technologies.

title: Mamata Banerjee raises monthly honorarium for priests, muezzins ahead of EC's poll schedule announcement
content: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee announced a Rs 500 hike in monthly honorariums for priests and muezzins, raising their pay to Rs 2,000. This move, along with the approval of all new applications, was made just before the Election Commission declared poll dates. Banerjee stated the decision recognizes the vital role of religious functionaries in community life.

title: Pakistan says it hit military facilities inside Afghanistan​, attacks tunnel used by Taliban
content: Pakistan launched strikes on Afghan military facilities and alleged terrorist hideouts in Kandahar, escalating tensions with the Taliban.  These actions follow Pakistan's interception of drone attacks originating from Afghanistan.  The Taliban administration reported limited civilian damage, while Pakistan warned of retaliation after its president stated a "red line" was crossed by targeting civilians.

title: 'I have no interest': BMC removes red-blue beacon from Mumbai mayor Ritu Tawde's car
content: Mumbai Mayor Ritu Tawde's official vehicle and escort car had their red and blue flashing lights removed following a social media controversy and an RTI activist's objection. The BMC confirmed similar lights on the deputy mayor's and leader of the house's vehicles were also taken down, citing an administrative lapse.

title: NHAI increases FASTag Annual Pass fee: Here's how much users have to pay from April 1
content: The National Highways Authority of India is increasing the FASTag Annual Pass fee. Starting April 1, 2026, the pass will cost Rs 3,075. This prepaid toll plan is for non-commercial private vehicles. The revision aligns with National Highways Fee Rules. Over 56 lakh users have adopted the pass. The new rate applies to eligible vehicles at approximately 1,150 fee plazas.

title: 'Phone pakad, social media hata': Abhishek Sharma reveals tough advice from Surya, Gambhir, Pandya
content: Abhishek Sharma faced a challenging start to the T20 World Cup 2026, scoring three consecutive ducks. Supported by captain Suryakumar Yadav and coach Gautam Gambhir, who advised him to disconnect from social media, Sharma regained his form. He played a crucial role in India's title win with a blistering 52 in the final.

title: 'Assaulted and abused her over caste': Bengaluru man kills live-in partner
content: A 23-year-old woman was allegedly murdered by her live-in partner in Bengaluru, who then attempted to stage it as a suicide. Police investigation revealed strangulation, leading to the accused's arrest. The victim had previously complained of frequent assaults and caste-based abuse by her partner. A case has been registered under murder and SC/ST Act provisions.

title: Middle East conflict forces Indian exporters to reroute shipments, absorb higher costs
content: Indian exporters are navigating Middle East conflict fallout, facing rising freight, insurance, and transit times. Businesses are adapting by adjusting inventory and contracts, while shipping lines reroute vessels around Africa, adding significant delays and costs. The government is exploring support measures, including airlifting perishables and alternative port options.

title: Opposition questions EC’s neutrality ahead of state assembly schedule announcement
content: The Election Commission is set to announce the schedule for assembly elections in Assam, Kerala, Puducherry, Tamil Nadu, and West Bengal today at 4 pm. Ahead of the announcement, opposition leaders have voiced concerns, questioning the poll body's neutrality and the effectiveness of the Model Code of Conduct.

title: 'We wanted more firepower': Gautam Gambhir explains bold Sanju Samson move in T20 World Cup
content: India's head coach Gautam Gambhir revealed Sanju Samson's T20 World Cup return was driven by an aggressive batting philosophy, not match-ups. The team aimed for more explosive powerplay starts, emphasizing Samson's game-changing ability. Gambhir also backed opener Abhishek Sharma, urging him to remain fearless and committed to his attacking approach.

title: Middle East War Day 16: UAE and other Gulf countries report fresh attacks; Kuwait airport hit
content: Explosions rocked Dubai as Iran claimed missile and drone strikes on the UAE's Al Dhafra airbase. Kuwait International Airport also reported drone impacts damaging radar, though no injuries occurred.  These actions follow Iran's warnings and accusations of UAE ports being used for attacks on its oil facilities, escalating regional tensions.

title: 'Will shoot you in head': Rapper Badshah gets death threat from Lawrence Bishnoi gang
content: Rapper Badshah has reportedly received a death threat from individuals linked to the Lawrence Bishnoi gang, warning him over his controversial song 'Tateeree'. The threat references a previous nightclub incident and comes after the Haryana State Commission for Women ordered his arrest for failing to appear before them regarding the song's objectionable lyrics.

title: From Bridgerton balls to fake shaadis: How theme parties are the new rage
content: Theme parties are transforming social gatherings into immersive experiences, driven by social media and a desire for participation. Brands are engineering these events to foster deeper engagement, turning passive fandom into collective celebration. This trend taps into psychological needs for connection and belonging, creating shared cultural moments that extend beyond mere entertainment.

title: EC to announce assembly poll dates for four states, one UT today
content: The Election Commission is set to announce the schedule for assembly elections in West Bengal, Assam, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, and Puducherry today. The upcoming polls are expected to involve fewer phases, particularly in West Bengal, with a substantial increase in central forces deployment. Supplementary voter lists for West Bengal will also be published following the adjudication of 'doubtful' voter cases.

title: Inside Hyderabad farmhouse: Surprise raid exposes politicians, businessmen at drug party
content: A surprise raid at a Moinabad farmhouse, attended by politicians and businessmen, led to the detention of ten individuals. Preliminary drug tests revealed five positive results, including former BRS MLA Pilot Rohit Reddy and TDP MP Putta Mahesh Kumar. Cocaine was reportedly recovered, and a dramatic incident involved a gunshot during the police operation.

title: Speeding SUV hits woman in Navi Mumbai, drags her 50m before crashing into 6 parked vehicles
content: A speeding SUV driven by a teenager, allegedly under the influence of alcohol, struck a woman and dragged her 50 meters before crashing into multiple vehicles. Prompt medical attention saved the woman's life, and police have registered an FIR against the driver and his chauffeur.

title: Nepal bus accident: 7 Indian pilgrims killed after vehicle plunges into valley
content: Tragedy struck in Nepal as a passenger bus carrying Indian pilgrims veered off a mountain road, plunging into a valley. At least seven Indians lost their lives, with nine others injured. The pilgrims were returning from the revered Manakamana Temple when the devastating accident occurred near Shahid Lakhan village.

title: Chinmayi Sripada slams Vairamuthu’s Jnanpith honour
content: Veteran Tamil poet Vairamuthu's Jnanpith Award win has ignited controversy. Singer Chinmayi Sripada, who previously accused him of sexual misconduct, voiced strong criticism, questioning the accolades from industry figures like Rajinikanth and Kamal Haasan. She highlighted the impact of #MeToo allegations and the silencing of victims, expressing disappointment over the recognition.

title: No captaincy for Virat Kohli, Watson snubbed in Dinesh Karthik's all-time RCB XI
content: Dinesh Karthik unveiled his all-time Royal Challengers Bengaluru XI, surprisingly naming Rajat Patidar as captain over Virat Kohli. Karthik lauded Patidar's leadership and impact, while also crediting Krunal Pandya's crucial role in their maiden IPL title win last season. RCB now prepares to defend their championship in IPL 2026 with a confident squad.

title: 'Slapped, threatened': Delivery boys assaulted amid rush for LPG cylinders in Pune
content: Cylinder delivery personnel in Pune faced aggression, with one slapped and another hit near the neck amidst a surge in cooking gas demand. Delivery boys were threatened and manhandled by customers demanding cylinders without bookings, leading to security concerns. Police are now coordinating with distributors to address these incidents.

title: Cocaine bust at farmhouse: After Rohith Reddy, TDP MP Putta Mahesh Kumar tests positive
content: Following a drug bust at a Moinabad farmhouse, former BRS MLA Pilot Rohith Reddy and TDP MP Putta Mahesh Kumar tested positive for drug use. Police seized cocaine and detained ten individuals, including the two politicians and a former Rajasthan MLA, after facing initial resistance during the raid.

title: Trump’s forced labour probe: China faces closest scrutiny; what does it mean for India?
content: India's solar panel, electronics, and garment exports face heightened US scrutiny following a new trade probe into forced labor in global supply chains. The investigation, covering 60 economies including India, examines goods made with forced labor or using inputs from such sources. This could impact Indian exporters reliant on Chinese components, demanding stricter documentation and compliance.

title: 100 stray dogs poisoned to death, buried near river in Telangana
content: Around 100 stray dogs were allegedly poisoned and killed in Kishtapur village, Mancherial district, on the alleged orders of the village sarpanch and gram panchayat secretary. A complaint filed on March 10 led to an FIR after intervention from the DGP. Animal activists are demanding exhumation and post-mortems for justice.

title: How Sheikh Sajjad Gul and Shabbir Lone rebuilt terror networks in Pak, B'desh
content: A 2002 Delhi Police operation arrested three Lashkar-e-Taiba operatives, leading to the neutralization of two commanders. Two of the arrested, Sajjad Gul and Shabbir Lone, later escaped India after serving their sentences. They have since resurfaced abroad, with Gul heading a LeT proxy and Lone allegedly running a terror network in Bangladesh.

title: Rohit Sharma on Mumbai vs rest debate: 'I don't believe in this edge'
content: Former India captain Rohit Sharma lauded the men's and women's cricket teams for their recent World Cup victories, expressing hope they will maintain this winning momentum. He emphasized hard work and the contributions of "people behind the scenes" for the teams' successes. Sharma also encouraged T20 Mumbai league players to seize opportunities and praised the MCA for reviving the league.

title: On way to PM Modi’s rally, BJP workers target Bengal minister Shashi Panja’s Girish Park residence
content: State minister Shashi Panja sustained injuries when BJP supporters en route to PM Modi's rally allegedly attacked her Kolkata home. The incident saw clashes between Trinamool and BJP workers, with police also injured. Panja accused BJP of violence, while the party claimed their buses were stoned first. The Election Commission has sought a report.

title: Filmmaker Sanoj Mishra seeks probe into ‘Kumbh girl’ Monalisa Bhonsle’s age after marriage
content: Filmmaker Sanoj Mishra has demanded an investigation into the age of 'Kumbh girl' Monalisa Bhonsle following her recent marriage. Mishra alleges Monalisa was a minor during the Maha Kumbh and claims her age documents may have been altered. He plans to meet her family to demand a medical examination and threatens legal action if she is found to be underage.

title: 'Not ice and fire, we are fire and fire': Sanju Samson on partnership with Abhishek Sharma
content: Sanju Samson and Abhishek Sharma's explosive 98-run opening partnership powered India to a massive 255/5 in the T20 World Cup final. India secured a commanding 96-run victory, clinching their third T20 World Cup title and becoming the first host nation to defend the crown. Samson lauded their 'fire and fire' partnership, highlighting their natural understanding and bravery.

title: India’s second-home boom: Why it is a smart real estate investment option
content: India's second-home market is shifting towards strategic investments in popular leisure destinations like Goa and Alibaug by 2026. Wealthy individuals are prioritizing growth areas offering both lifestyle benefits and rental income potential. This trend is driven by improved infrastructure, a focus on wellness, and the rise of remote work, making these locations attractive for long-term appreciation and personal enjoyment.
